diagnosis_packet.problem_statement: Concurrent successful user loads should share one provider call. In production, a temporary provider failure is converted to a fallback and permanently poisons later loads for the same key. The caller must observe the original failure, and a later retry must be allowed to start fresh while successful in-flight requests remain coalesced.

conclusion.claim: The observed failure is primarily caused by state drift.
conclusion.reason: Deterministic evidence gate selected a stronger supported competing hypothesis.

contract_invariants: Failed per-key in-flight work must be evicted by the state owner; the original error remains observable, successful concurrent work stays coalesced, and retry must not await its own cached promise.

hypothesis_results.claim: The observed failure is primarily caused by dependency drift.
hypothesis_results.falsification: Hold every other dimension constant and show that changing dependency does not change the outcome.

hypothesis_results.falsification: Hold every other dimension constant and show that changing state does not change the outcome.

hypothesis_results.claim: The observed failure is primarily caused by code drift.
hypothesis_results.falsification: Hold every other dimension constant and show that changing code does not change the outcome.

diagnosis_packet.problem_statement: An aborted provider request continues through a wrapper with a detached signal and is retried as though it were a transient dependency error. Preserve normal retry behavior, but propagate the caller's AbortSignal through the adapter and stop retrying immediately when the dependency reports AbortError.

conclusion.claim: The observed failure is primarily caused by dependency drift.
conclusion.reason: Heuristic fallback only; a local investigator should challenge this ranking.

contract_invariants: Propagate the caller's exact cancellation signal through every wrapper; cancellation is terminal for retries and must use existing platform error identity without invented dependencies.

plan.analysis: The validation failure indicates that the caller's abort signal is not being propagated through the adapter, leading to a loss of signal identity. This prevents the retry mechanism from correctly handling the abort signal.

files.description: Modify the callProvider function to propagate the caller's AbortSignal directly instead of creating a detached one.
Ensure that the retryRequest function correctly propagates the abort signal to the adapter and stops retrying immediately if an AbortError is received.

plan.notes: The selected files are sufficient to address the issue without introducing unnecessary complexity or dependencies. The tests should be updated to validate the new behavior.

diagnosis_packet.problem_statement: A TTL cache accepts an injected clock for deterministic replay, yet expiration follows the host wall clock. Refreshing an existing key also retains its old deadline. Repair the clock and refresh contracts so replay time controls expiration and a refreshed value receives the new TTL.

plan.analysis: The previous patch failed validation because it did not address the root cause of the issue. The failure is primarily caused by data drift and requires a more comprehensive approach to aggregate independent one-to-many relationships without causing a Cartesian multiplication.

files.description: Aggregate each independent child relation to its parent key before joining sibling one-to-many data.

plan.notes: The query needs to be modified to ensure that each independent child relation (fills and fees) is aggregated to its parent key before joining sibling one-to-many data. This will prevent a Cartesian multiplication and ensure accurate aggregation of quantities and fees.

files.description: Aggregate each independent child relation to its parent key before joining sibling one-to-many data.

plan.notes: The query needs to be modified to ensure that each independent child relation is aggregated to its parent key before joining sibling one-to-many data. This will prevent a Cartesian multiplication and ensure that the account report is correct when each order has one fill and one fee, but overstates both quantity and fees when either side has multiple child rows.
